remarks.text: Multi-Layer Policy for Securing AI Agents
remarks.content: As part of our work at Tigera building products that create secure runtime environments for enterprise agents at scale in the real world, one small part of this puzzle I think about a lot is... The post Multi-Layer Policy for Securing AI Agents appeared first on Tigera – Creator of Calico. ]]>

remarks.name: Dataiku Blog
remarks.role: AI governance, enterprise AI platform deployment, MLOps, analytics, agent orchestration.
remarks.text: Finance analytics and AI: how decision intelligence platforms improve FP&A, risk, and reporting
remarks.content: Finance teams have more data than they can act on. Transactions stream in from enterprise resource planning (ERP), general ledger (GL), accounts payable and receivable (AP/AR), market feeds, and treasury systems around the clock. But by the time that data reaches a decision, the moment h
remarks.contentTranslation: 财务团队拥有的数据太多，无法采取行动。交易全天候从企业资源规划（ ERP ）、总分类账（ GL ）、应付账款和应收账款（ AP/AR ）、市场馈送和财务系统流入。但当数据做出决定时，时刻h
remarks.translation: 财务分析和AI：决策智能平台如何改善财务规划与资产管理、风险和报告

remarks.role: AI governance, enterprise AI platform deployment, MLOps, analytics, agent orchestration.
remarks.text: From partnership to pipeline: how Snowflake and Dataiku drove 200%+ YoY sourced opportunity growth
remarks.content: Partnerships are often judged too early, or on the wrong things. Visibility. Executive alignment. Event presence. A well-worded press release. None of that is the real test.
remarks.contentTranslation: 人们往往过早地判断伙伴关系，或者判断错误。可见性。高管一致性。事件存在。措辞良好的新闻稿。这些都不是真正的考验。
remarks.translation: 从合作伙伴关系到渠道： Snowflake和Dataiku如何推动200% +同比机会增长

remarks.role: AI governance, enterprise AI platform deployment, MLOps, analytics, agent orchestration.
remarks.text: Alteryx data analytics in 2026: strengths, limitations, and alternatives
remarks.content: In our view, Alteryx built its reputation on a simple promise: Let business analysts do data work that used to require SQL, Python, or a request to IT. For years, that promise held. Drag-and-drop workflows replaced manual spreadsheet processes. Teams that waited weeks for data engineerin
remarks.contentTranslation: 我们认为， Alteryx的声誉建立在一个简单的承诺之上：让业务分析师做过去需要SQL、Python或IT请求的数据工作。多年来，这个承诺一直保持着。拖放工作流取代了手动电子表格流程。等待数据工程师数周的团队
remarks.translation: 2026年Alteryx数据分析：优势、局限性和替代方案

remarks.name: Peter Yang
remarks.role: Practical AI tutorials and interviews to help busy people become AI builders
remarks.text:
What is Google’s equivalent (or up and coming competitor) of Codex and Claude Code? 

If it’s Antigravity, should that be part of Gemini?

This stuff is going to merge very fast like ChatGPT / Codex being able to do coding, knowledge work, basic Q&A, and much more from any device.

Hoping Google is working on a good solution here.
remarks.translation: Google的Codex和Claude Code的等效（或未来的竞争对手）是什么？如果是反重力，那应该是双子座的一部分吗？这些东西将很快合并，就像ChatGPT/Codex能够从任何设备进行编码、知识工作、基本问答等等。希望谷歌正在研究一个好的解决方案。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 AI 编程工具、设计到代码流程和软件生产方式的变化；需要结合产品发布或文档再做事实判断。

remarks.name: Aaron Levie
remarks.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
remarks.text:
There’s no amount of intelligence that can get packed into AI models that replaces the need for context. For any sufficiently general purpose AI, you will always have to guide it in the direction you want as it has an infinite range of directions it can go in.

As long as the same model is used by a lawyer, an engineer, a financial analyst, or a healthcare professional, and as long as you’re trying to do anything uniquely differentiated or specific, then instructions, domain context, and proprietary data will always need to get into the context window for the model to be useful.

This is partly why AI automation doesn’t come for free, and why there’s still a wide spectrum of who’s getting the largest gains from AI and who’s not. You have to put in real work, and you get real value on the other end.

This is one of the advantages that applied AI will also have in the market. Any layer of abstraction above just the raw intelligence that can meaningfully get you off to the races faster will likely continue to be valuable.
remarks.translation:
没有多少智能可以集成到AI模型中，取代对上下文的需求。对于任何足够通用的AI，你总是必须引导它朝着你想要的方向前进，因为它有无限的方向。
只要律师、工程师、金融分析师或医疗保健专业人员使用相同的模型，并且只要您尝试做任何独特或特定的事情，那么指令、领域上下文和专有数据将始终需要进入上下文窗口，以使模型有用。
这就是为什么AI自动化不是免费的，以及为什么仍然有很多人从AI中获得最大的收益，而谁没有。你必须投入真正的工作，并在另一端获得真正的价值。这是应用AI在市场上也将具有的优势之一。除了原始情报之外的任何抽象层都可以让你更快地进入比赛，这可能仍然是有价值的。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察模型运行、算力、存储、sandbox 或开发平台基础设施的变化；后续事实判断需回到原始公告或技术文档。

remarks.name: Nikunj Kothari
remarks.role: partner fpvventures - investing in seed/A. previous: early hire meter, opendoor, atlassian & others. love shimoleejhaveri + 👦👧
remarks.text:
The funniest texts are from founders who meet “thesis driven” GPs hoping they’ll understand EXACTLY what they are building.. 

And then realize the thesis was written and built by an associate (or, worse an intern). 

Don’t always read what the VC writes on the internet - yes, that includes me too (although I can guarantee I don’t have an associate, intern, EA or ghostwriter) 😆
remarks.translation: 最有趣的文本来自创始人，他们遇到了“论文驱动”的全科医生，希望他们能确切地了解他们正在构建什么。然后意识到论文是由同事（或者更糟糕的是实习生）撰写和构建的。不要总是阅读VC在互联网上写的内容-是的，这也包括我（尽管我可以保证我没有助理、实习生、EA或代笔作家） 😆
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Swyx
remarks.role: achieve ambition with intentionality, intensity, integrity & insanity.
remarks.text:
It's finally out!!! @METR_Evals found that more than half of SWEBench results is unmergeable slop.  FrontierCode represents over 1000+ hours of maintainer validated software engineering work most frontier models cannot yet solve, much less solve with high quality.

Cog had IOI Gold medalists and top code maintainers Look At The Data — FrontierCode includes 3000+ rubrics covering code quality and anticheat reward hacking plaguing other benchmarks. 

FC Diamond is so hard that Opus 4.8 scores 13.8%.

Three eras of AI coding : Three eras of benchmarks

2021 • Autocomplete : HumanEval
2023 • Passing Tests: SWEBench, TerminalBench
2026 • Maintainable Code: FrontierCode

to me the most beautiful chart when I requested a special historical run into all extant old models, the data was finding that the easiest third of FC tasks (in FC Extended) were rapidlly and suddenly solved over late 2025 - Opus almost doubled from a 41% pass rate to 74% in 4 months. 

This describes the "WTF happened in Dec 2025" vibe shift that a lot of folks from @dhh to @karpathy have called out: it is the difference between getting 95% success in 2 rerolls vs 6, making it finally feasible to go up the next layer of abstraction in agentic coding, eg @GeoffreyHuntley's ralph loops or @bcherny's /goals or  @steipete's "loops that prompt your agents" without fearing too much that things go off the rails.

My guess: as AI accelerates from here, each FrontierCode tier will saturate in sequence, hopefully ~annually. I've already asked the team to prepare FrontierCode 2027....

The old mountains will be destroyed. Their rubble becomes regolith. And from that regolith, the next model forest grows. Circle of life.
remarks.translation:
终于出来了！ ！ ！ @ METR_Evals发现，超过一半的SWEBench结果是不可合并的SLOP。FrontierCode代表了大多数前沿模型无法解决的超过1000小时的维护人员验证软件工程工作，更不用说高质量的解决方案了。Cog让IOI金牌得主和顶级代码维护人员查看数据— FrontierCode包括3000多个涵盖代码质量和反热奖励黑客攻击的标准，困扰着其他基准。
FC Diamond是如此坚硬，以至于Opus 4.8得分为13.8%。
AI编码的三个时代： 2021年基准的三个时代•自动完成： HumanEval 2023 •通过测试： SWEBench ， TerminalBench 2026 •可维护代码：当我请求对所有现存的旧模型进行特殊历史运行时， FrontierCode对我来说是最美丽的图表，数据发现，最简单的三分之一的FC任务（在FC Extended中）在2025年末迅速并突然得到解决- OPUS在4个月内通过率几乎翻了一番，从41 ％增加到74 ％。
这描述了“WTF发生在2025年12月”的氛围转变，从@ dhh到@ karpathy ，很多人都在呼唤：这是在2次重播与6次重播中取得95%成功之间的区别，这使得在Agent编码中最终可以进行下一层抽象，例如@ GeoffreyHuntley的ralph循环或@ bcherny的/goals或@ steipete的“提示Agent的循环” ，而不必担心事情会越轨。
我觉得想：随着AI从这里加速，每个FrontierCode层都将按顺序饱和，希望~每年。我已经要求团队准备好FrontierCode 2027....旧山将被摧毁。他们的废墟变成了风化层。从那个风化层开始，下一个模型森林生长。生命的循环。
remarks.observation: 这条言论可作为 Agent 产品形态、协作方式或能力边界的观察入口；只代表公开观点，不作为公司事实主证据。

remarks.name: Peter Yang
remarks.role: Practical AI tutorials and interviews to help busy people become AI builders
remarks.text: Feels like there’s a completely different set of best practices for AI builders on the $200 / month subsidized subscriptions vs employees working at companies that are trying not to overspend API costs
remarks.translation: 对于每月200 $的补贴订阅，与在试图不超支API成本的公司工作的员工相比，AI开发人员有一套完全不同的最佳实践

remarks.name: Josh Woodward
remarks.role: VP, Google GoogleLabs GeminiApp GoogleAIStudio
remarks.text:
The new killer NotebookLM feature: easily being able to expand your search beyond your own source files

Then, with today's update, you can also make new output formats: PDFs, DOCX, XLSX, PPTX, charts, etc.

We want NotebookLM to keep helping you do better research https://t.co/4TCw7tQ2GV
remarks.translation: 新的杀手级NotebookLM功能：轻松将搜索扩展到您自己的源文件之外。然后，通过今天的更新，您还可以制作新的输出格式： PDF、DOCX、XLSX、PPTX、图表等。我们希望NotebookLM继续帮助您进行更好的研究

remarks.name: Boris Cherny
remarks.role: Claude Code anthropicai
remarks.text:
When we first demoed Claude Code internally, it got two reactions on Slack. 

A year after GA, @_catwu and I sat down to talk about what's changed: why I use auto mode instead of plan mode, how routines fix bugs before I see them, why I do most of my coding from my phone now, and where the product is going
remarks.translation: 当我们首次在内部演示Claude Code时，它在Slack上引起了两种反应。GA一年后， @_catwu和我坐下来讨论了发生了什么变化：为什么我使用自动模式而不是计划模式，例程如何在我看到它们之前修复错误，为什么我现在用手机进行大部分编码，以及产品的发展方向
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 AI 编程工具、设计到代码流程和软件生产方式的变化；需要结合产品发布或文档再做事实判断。

remarks.name: Garry Tan
remarks.role: President & CEO ycombinator —Founder garryslist—Creator of GStack & GBrain—designer/engineer who helps founders—SF Dem accelerating the boom loop
remarks.text:
Because this is a brand new form of centrism being born in San Francisco

The 2030’s will look back on this time when the new San Francisco common sense Democrat was born from the failures of the hard left https://t.co/fxkV2LozeF
remarks.translation: 因为这是在旧金山诞生的一种全新的中立主义形式。2030年代将回顾这个时代，当时新的旧金山常识民主党人从强硬左翼的失败中诞生

remarks.name: Nikunj Kothari
remarks.role: partner fpvventures - investing in seed/A. previous: early hire meter, opendoor, atlassian & others. love shimoleejhaveri + 👦👧
remarks.text:
Fun to see all the “autonomous” companies being launched in the late few months.. however, even with all the loops, the last mile is still quite hard. 

That gap probably shrinks in the next few months! https://t.co/R7L8TDFuvC
remarks.translation: 有趣的是，所有的“自主”公司都在最近几个月推出……然而，即使有了所有的循环，最后一英里仍然相当艰难。在接下来的几个月里，这一差距可能会缩小！
